name: Slice & Bake Oatmeal Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut four 14" x 12-inch pieces of waxed paper or plastic wrap.
2. Cream butter or margarine and sugars in a large bowl until smooth.
3. Beat in eggs and vanilla until light and fluffy.
4. In a medium bowl, combine dry ingredients (flour, rolled oats, salt, baking soda, baking powder).
5. Stir the flour mixture into the egg mixture until blended.
6. Stir in nuts and raisins.
7. Divide dough into 4 pieces.
8. Shape each piece into an 8 to 10 inch roll.
9. Wrap each roll in one piece of waxed paper or plastic wrap.
10. Place rolls in a freezer container with a tight-fitting lid or wrap airtight in foil.
11. Label and date the container.
12. Store in the freezer for up to 6 months.
To make cookies:
1. Slightly thaw dough roll at room temperature.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F.
3. Lightly grease baking sheets; set aside.
4. Cut dough into slices or chunks.
5. Place dough about 1 inch apart on prepared baking sheets.
6. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until edges are light brown and centers are slightly set.
7. Cool about 2 minutes on the baking sheet.
8. Remove cookies and cool on wire racks.
